description: Canonical cross-language parity vectors for the typeid primitive. Every SDK shipped by kit (go/core/id, sdk/experimental/rs, sdk/ts, sdk/py, sdk/experimental/php) MUST produce the listed `typeid` string when fed `(prefix, uuid)`, and MUST round-trip `typeid` back to `(prefix, uuid)`. A divergence is a wire-format break. Capability flags (skip_in) document SDK-specific opt-outs verified empirically against the upstream libs (jetify/typeid, mti, typeid-js, typeid-python, jewei/typeid-php).

vectors.name: empty-prefix-bare-suffix
vectors.note: The Rust kit wrapper (sdk/experimental/rs) rejects empty prefix in id::new and id::from_uuid as a kit-level policy. The bare-suffix form is still valid per the Jetify v0.3 spec and supported by go/ts/py/php — see ADR 0001.
